prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_svr', seasonality_champion='FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extremely high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.17) and very high seasonality variance (0.76), indicating models capture patterns fundamentally differently. FT_svr dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error) and seasonality correlation, while FT_rf achieves the best overall RMSE. The mixed tier structure with early/late horizon specialists suggests DBA combination is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "late": "FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "rf", "THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 2.1736,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extremely high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.17) and very high seasonality variance (0.76), indicating models capture patterns fundamentally differently. FT_svr dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error) and seasonality correlation, while FT_rf achieves the best overall RMSE. The mixed tier structure with early/late horizon specialists suggests DBA combination is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=3 suggests per-horizon selection is valuable. relative_spread_mean=7.23 indicates high model disagreement favoring dba_combination (recommended_method_hint). RMSE spread ratio=2.17 with n_windows=3 requires shrinkage≥0.3 on inverse_rmse_weights. FT_svr dominates trend/seasonality but FT_rf has best overall RMSE (8734 vs 30491 for worst), so diversification across selection, robust baseline, and weighted methods is optimal."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extremely high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.17) and very high seasonality variance (0.76), indicating models capture patterns fundamentally differently. FT_svr dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error) and seasonality correlation, while FT_rf achieves the best overall RMSE. The mixed tier structure with early/late horizon specialists suggests DBA combination is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=3 suggests per-horizon selection is valuable. relative_spread_mean=7.23 indicates high model disagreement favoring dba_combination (recommended_method_hint). RMSE spread ratio=2.17 with n_windows=3 requires shrinkage≥0.3 on inverse_rmse_weights. FT_svr dominates trend/seasonality but FT_rf has best overall RMSE (8734 vs 30491 for worst), so diversification across selection, robust baseline, and weighted methods is optimal.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity (selection=best_per_horizon_by_validation, baseline=dba_combination, weighted=inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). No leakage detected - validation_summary.n_windows=3 with expanding mode suggests anti-leakage strategies. Universe leaderboards show current candidates outperform universe ranking_top (e.g., inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 score=0.836 vs exp_weighted_average_eta0.5_trim0.8 score=0.768). No redundancy or pruning needed.
